commands.review_summary:
The PR adds durable cursor rotation for untargeted proof-nudges and bot-proof scans, wires cursor paths and state publishing into the scheduled workflow, documents the behavior, and adds focused tests.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main always begins from the same sorted proof-lane prefix and stops at processedLimit, so skip-heavy early candidates can consume repeated scheduled scan windows. I did not run a live scheduled workflow.

target.title: [codex] fix: rotate proof nudge scans
target.body:
## What Changed

- Added durable cursor rotation for untargeted `proof-nudges` and `bot-proof` scans.
- Wired the scheduled proof-nudge workflow to pass cursor paths, publish cursor state back to `clawsweeper-state`, and accept an optional `processed_limit` / `CLAWSWEEPER_PROOF_NUDGES_PROCESSED_LIMIT` override.
- Documented the cursor behavior and added focused coverage for candidate rotation, cursor persistence, and workflow wiring.

## Why

The scheduled proof-nudge lane could spend its processed-record budget on the same skip-heavy prefix. When that happened, later stale proof candidates had to wait even though the workflow was running. The cursor now advances to the last processed candidate after execute runs, so the next scheduled pass starts on the next bounded window.

Targeted `--item-numbers` runs remain deterministic and do not read or update cursors.

## Validation

- `pnpm run build:all`
- `pnpm run lint:src`
- `pnpm run lint:scripts`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/clawsweeper.ts test/proof-nudge-policy.test.ts test/sweep-workflow.test.ts docs/proof-nudges.md .github/workflows/proof-nudges.yml`
- `node --test test/proof-nudge-policy.test.ts`
- `node --test --test-name-pattern "proof nudge workflow" test/sweep-workflow.test.ts`
target.author: brokemac79
